acceptance_criteria: Backup files created with correct naming.
prd_refs: §9.8 `Set-SlashOpsConfig` requires backup before write and restore on invalid result.
description: Provide a backup helper for config and policy mutations. Every `Set-`, `Edit-`, `Reset-`, or `Invoke-ConfigMigration` call must pass through this helper to write a timestamped copy under `~/.slashops/backups/` before touching the live file. On post-write validation failure, the caller restores from the backup.
notes: Use `Copy-Item` rather than reading + writing to avoid encoding drift.
Backups directory must be created lazily — first call creates it.
steps: TDD: Backup creates `~/.slashops/backups/{filename}.{timestamp}.{schemaVersion}.json` with original content.
TDD: Timestamp uses `yyyy-MM-ddTHHmmss` in local time, matching PRD §9.10 example.
TDD: Helper is idempotent for the same file within the same second (suffix bumped).
TDD: Helper returns the backup path so callers can restore.
TDD: Restore-from-backup helper recreates the original file atomically.
Author `Private/Config/ConvertTo-ConfigBackup.ps1` taking `(Path)` and returning the backup path.
Add a `Restore-ConfigBackup` companion (private) that copies a backup back into place.
Pester tests cover every TDD bullet using `TestDrive:`.
name: [cfg-05] Backup-before-write helper for config files
